rationale: Section 11.26(a-10)(4) dynamically references the §11.13(b) exemption amount in its limitation formula. No amendment to §11.26 is needed; the formula automatically incorporates the increased exemption, though transition-year calculations will change for newly qualifying individuals.
quote: (4) multiplying the amount of any increase in the current tax year as compared to the preceding tax year in the aggregate amount of the exemptions to which the individual is entitled under Sections 11.13(b) and (c) by the school district's tax rate for the current tax year; and
_note: No direct conflict. §11.26 references §11.13(b) by citation rather than hardcoding the dollar amount, so the limitation formula self-adjusts. However, a $60,000 exemption increase will materially alter the (a-10)(4) subtraction for individuals newly qualifying in the transition year, reducing their school-tax limitation amount. Agencies may need implementation guidance.
